ORDINANCE NO. #SEU(NEW SERIES)
AN ORDINANCE nunNDIno THE SAN DIEGO COUNTY
CODE OF REGULATORY ORDIRACES BY REPEALING
CHAPTER 9 (coumchIxs mm SECTION 32.901)
0F DIVISION 2, TITLE 3 AND BY ADDING THERETO
A NEW CHAPTER 9 AConENcINc wlTN SECTION
32.901) RELnTlNG Tc ELFCTION chrwnIcN
FINANCE AND CONTROL
The Board of Supervisors of the County of San Diego
do ordain as follows:
Section 1.
chapter 9 (commencing with Section 32.901)
Division 2, Title 3, of the San Diego County Code of Regulatory
Ordinances is hereby repealed.
section 2,
chapter
9
(commencing with Section 32.90D
is hereby added to Division 2, Title 3 of the San Diego County
Code of Regulatory ordinances to read as follows:
CHAPTER 9
ELECTION CAMPAIGN
FINANCE AND coNTRoL
A.
GENERAL PROVISIONS
SEC. 32rSUl.
PURPOSE AND INTENT
Inherent in the high cost of election campaigning is
the problem of improper influence, real or potential, excercised
by campaign contributors over elected officials.
It is the
purpose and intent of the Board of Supervisors of the County
of San Diego in enacting this chapter to preserve an orderly
political forun in whlch individuals may express themselves
effectively: to place realistic and enforceable limits on
the amounts of money that may be contributed to political
campaigns in county elections; to prohibit contrioutions
by organizations in order to develop a broader nase OE political
efficacy within the cov“;hitv; to limit the use of loans
and credit in the findLClng of county election cangaigns;
and to provide full and fair enforcement of all the provisions
of this chapter.
8/9/77 (4])


SEC. 32.906.
"CozulITTEE"
Whenever used in this chapter, the term "committee
shall mean any person or combination of persons vho directly
or indirectly receives contributions or makes expenditures
or contributions for the purpose of influencing or attenptinq
to influence the action of the voters for or against the
nomination or election of one or more candidates, or the
passage or defeat of any County measure, including any
committee or subcommittee of a political party, whether
national, state or local, if:
(1)
Contributions received total five
hundred dollars
($500)
or morc in a calendar year:
(2)
Expenditures and contributions made
other than contributions described in subsection (3),
total five hundred dollars
($500)
or more in a
calendar year; or
r
(3)
contributions of cash, checks and other
cash equivalents paid directly to candidates and
-
committees total five thousand dollars
(55.00m
or more in a calendar year
SEC. 32.907.
"CONTRquTIoN"
Whenever used in this chapter, the term "contribution"
shall mean a payment, a forgiveness of a loan, a payment of
a loan by a third party, or an enforceable promise to make a
payment except to the extent that full and adequate consideration
is received unless it is clear from the surrounding circumstances
that it is not made for political purposes.
An expenditure
made at the behest of a candidate, committee or elected office:
is a contribution to the candidate, committee or elected
officer unless full and adequate consideration is received
for making the expenditure.
The term "contribution" shall include the purchase
of tickets for events such as dinners, luncheons, rallies
and similar fund raising events;
a candidate's own money
or property used on behalf of his candidacy; the granting
of credit in the normal course of business;
the granting
of discounts or rebates not extended to the public generally
or the qrantinq of discounts or rebates by television and
radio stations and newspapers not extended on on equal basis
to all Canaldntss {or the some of‘ira; and the payment of
compensation by any person for the personal services or
expenses of any other person if such services are rendered
or expenses incurred on behalf of a candidate or committee
without payment of full and adequate consideration.
-3-


(2)
"County measure"
includes any measure under
subdivision (1) of this subsection which is submitted
to a popular vote at an election by action of the eoard
of Supznlisors,
or which is
submitted or
is
intendcd
to be
sub'litted to
a popular vote at an election
by initiative, referendum, or recall procedure. whether
or not it qualifies for the ballot.
SEC.
32.91].
"ELECTIVE COUNTY OFFICE”
whenever used in this chapter, the term "Elective County
office" shall mean:
Member or the Board or supervisors
Assessor; County Clerk; District Attorney; Recorder; sheriff:
Treasurer—Tax Collector or member of
the County Board of
Educationi
sac. 32.912.
"axrohcslmnw AUTHORITY“
'
Whenever used in this chapter, the term "enforcement
authority“ shall mean the District Attorney of the county of
San Diego. Nothing in this chapter shall be construed as
limiting the authority of any law enforcement agency or
prosecuting attorney to enforce the provisions of this
chapter under any circumstances where such law enforcement
agency or prosecuting attorney otherwise has lawful authority
to do so.
.
sec. 32.913.
"EXPENDITURE"
'
Whenever used in
this chapter,
the term
"Expenditure"
shall mean a payment, a forgiveness of a loan, a payment of
a loan by a third party, or an enforceable promise to make a
payment, unless it is clear from the surrounding circumstances
that it is not made for political purposes.
An expenditure
is made on the date the payment is made or on the date
consideration,
if any, is received, whichever is earlier.
sac. 32.914.
"INDEPENDENT EXPENDITURE"
whenever used in this chapter,
the term "independent
expenditure" shall mean an expenditure by a person for a
communication expressly advocating the support of or opposition
to
a clearly identified Candidate or measure which is not
made with the cooperation or with the prior consent of, a
candidate or his agent or a controlled committee of a candidate
or measure.
For the purposes of this definition:
ts-


association, committee, labor union, and any other organization
or group of persons acting in concert.
C.
CAMPAIGN TREASURER
szc. 32.917.
CAMPAIGN CONTRIBUTION CHECKING ACCOUNT
Every candidate and every committee shall establish one
campaign contribution checking account at an office of a
bank located in the county of san niego,
within 24 hours aiter
openina the account, the candidate or the committee shall file
with the Reqistrar of Voters and the rnforcencnt Authority
the name and address of the bank and the number of the account.
All contributions of money or checks, or anything or value
converted by such candidate or committee to money or a
check, made to a candidate, to a person on behalf of a
candidate, to a committee supporting or opposing a candidate
or a county measure, or to a person on behalf of a committee
supporting or opposing a candidate or a county noasure shall
be placed in the candidate's or conmittee's checking account
within three
(37 business days.
A candidate and a committee
'
(armed solely for the purpose of acting in aid of the nomination
,
or election or the candidate may establish and maintain one
joint checking account [or the candidate and the committee,
sac. 32.918.
DISDURSEHEHTS
'
Funds shall be disbursed iron a campaign contribution
checking account established pursuant to section 32.917
hereof only by check signed by the candidate or by the
candidate's or committee's campaign treasurer or designated
agents of the campaign treasurer.
Each candidate or Committee
shall maintain a written record of the payee at each check
drawn on a campaign contribution checking account and an
itemized record of the goods or services for which each
check is issued.
shci 32‘919.
FETTY CASH FUND
section 32.913 notwithstanding, the candidate or campaign
treasurer or other designated agents authorized to issue
checks on a campaign contribution checking account may
disburse to the candidate or committee establishing the
checking account an amount not greater than $20 per week to
be used for petty cash purDoGeS by the candidate or committee
An amount not greater than sea per vnak ray b2 disbu:sed to
a candidate and committee jointly establishing a campaign
contribution checking account, to be used for petty cash
purposes.
-7-


(b)
No person shall make, and no campaign treasurer
shall solicit or accept, any contribution whicn will cause
the total amount contributed by such person with respect
to a sinqle election in support of or opposition to a County
measure to Exceed five hundred dollars
($500).
(c)
ritcnsions of credit for a period of more than
thirty
(30} days are prohibited,
Extensions of credit of
more than two hundred fifty dollars
($250) are prohibited.
Provided, however, a candidate may personally torrow an
unlimited amount and such funds shall be considered as a
contribution by the candidate himself; provided, further
that such transaction is fully disclosed and documented in
accordance with applicable law.
(d)
The terms of this section are applicable to any
contributions made to a candidate or committee hereunder,
whether used by such candidate or committee to finance a
current campaign, to pay deficits incurred in prior campaigns,
or otherwise.
(e)
if any person is found guilty of violating the
terms of this section, the amount of funds received constituting
such violation shall be paid by the candidate or committee
treasurer who received such funds to the county Treasurer for
deposit in the General Fund of the County.
'
scc. 37t924.
ORGANIZATIONAL CONTRIBUTIONS
(a)
No person, other than an individual, shall make
a contribution to any candidate or committee; provided
however, that this section shall not apply to contributions
made to a committee which is organized solely for the purpose
of supporting or opposing the qualification for the ballot
or adoption of one or more county measures.
.
(b)
No officer, employee, agent or attorney or other
representative of a person covered by this division shall
aid, abet, advise or participate in a violation of this section
(c)
No person shall knowingly accept a payment or contri—
bution made in violation of this section.
(d)
K’ a camcnign treasurer is offered a cor+ribution
which would be in excess of the limitation, the treasnter
must refuse the contribution.
If, however, a contribution
which is in violation of this section is deposited into the
campaign trust account, he shall report in writing within five
(5) days or the receipt of the contribution to the Enforcement
Authority the facts surrounding such payment or contribution.


SEC.
32.923.
ADVERTISING RATES; SERVICE reds hen CImRGBS
To the cxtcnt that any person sells space in any newspaper
or magazine or sells time on a broadcast station to a candidate
or committee or performs other services in connection with
the campaign of the candidate or for or against the measure,
the charecs made [or the use 0( such space or time shall not
exceed the charges normally made for comparable use of such
space or time by other users thereof,
SEC. 32.929.
SUPPLIERS 0F Goons amp snavrcus~rDISCLosuhg or
-
RECORDS REQUIRED
No person who supplies goods or services or both goods
and services to a candidate or committee for use in connection
with the campaign of the candidate or for or against the
measure shall refuse knowingly to divulge or disclose to the
Enforcement Authority his record of any expenditures made b1
the candidate or committee in payment for such goods or
services or both.
.
a.
OFFICIAL DUTIES
-
‘
an. 32.930.
DUTIES or REGISTRAR or voThRs
In addition to other duties required of him under the
terms of this chapter, the Registrar of Voters of the County
shall:
(a)
supply appropriate forms and manuals prescribed by
the California Fair Political Practices Commission.
These
forms and manuals shall be furnished to all candidates and
committees, and to all other persons required to report.
(b)
Determine whether required documents have been
filed and, if so, whether they conform on their face with
the requirements of state la»
la)
Notify promptly all persons and known committees
who have failed to file a document in the form and at the
time required by state law.
(d)
Report apparent violations of this chapter and
applicable state law to the anorcement Authority.
(c)
Compile and maintain a current list of all statements
or parts of steteaemt. {zltd with his office pertaining to
each candidate and each measure
(E)
Cooperate with the Enforcement Authority in the
performance of the duties of the anercement ruthority as
prescribed in this chapter and applicable state laws.
.11.


Size. 32,934.
mums or CONSTRUCTION
This chapter shall he construed liberally in order to
effectuate its purposes.
No error, irregularity, informality,
neglect or omission of any officer in any procedure taken
under this chapter which does not directly affect the jurisdiction
of the Connty to control Campaign contributions and expenditures
shall avoid the effect of this chapter.
sac. 32,935.
SEVERABILITY
_
If any section, subsection, subdivision, sentence,
clause, phrase, or portion of this chapter, or the application
thereof to any person,
if for any reason held to he invalid
or unconstitutional by the decision of any court of Competent
jurisdiction, such decision shall not affect Lhe validity of
the remaining portions of this chapter or its application to
other persons.
The Board of Supervisors herepy declares
that it would have adopted this chapter, and each section,
subsection,
subdivision,
sentence,
Clause: Phrase or portion
thereof, irrespective of the fact that any one or hate
,
sections, subsections, subdivisions, sentences, clauses.
phrases, or portions, or the application thereof to any
Person, be declared invalid or unconstitutional,
Section 3.
This ordinance shall take effect and he in
’ force thirty
(30) days after the date of its passage,- and
before the expiration of fifteen
(15) days after its passage
it shall be published once with the names of the nenhers
voting for and against the same in the
San Diego Daily Transcript
a newspaper of general circulation published in the County of
San Diego.
PASSED, APPROVED, AND ADOPTED this 9th day of August, 1977.

sections.question: In view of the limitations in the San Diego County Election Campaign Finance and Control Ordinance and the enactment of AB 1400, statutes of 1977, is a loan to a candidate or committee from other than a commercial lending institution subject to a limitation of $250 per election?
sections.question_synthetic: Whether loans to candidates or committees from non-commercial sources are subject to the $250 per election contribution limit under AB 1400 and the San Diego County ordinance.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Loans received from non-commercial lending institutions are treated as contributions under AB 1400 Section 84216 unless received from a commercial lending institution in the ordinary course of business and it is clear from circumstances they are not made for political purposes; such loans would therefore be subject to contribution limitations.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both an incoming request letter (January 20, 1978 from Registrar of Voters) and appears to be the beginning of the FPPC response. The response section is partially visible but truncated. Document also includes the full text of San Diego County Ordinance No. 8550 on election campaign finance. The actual FPPC conclusion/advice letter appears incomplete in the provided text. Confidence reduced due to document fragmentation and missing complete response section.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: In view of the limitations in the San Diego County Election Campaign Finance and Control Ordinance and the enactment of AB 1400, statutes of 1977, is a loan to a candidate or committee from other than a commercial lending institution subject to a limitation of $250 per election?

CONCLUSION: Loans received from non-commercial lending institutions are treated as contributions under AB 1400 Section 84216 unless received from a commercial lending institution in the ordinary course of business and it is clear from circumstances they are not made for political purposes; such loans would therefore be subject to contribution limitations.

embedding.summary: This FPPC advice letter responds to the San Diego County Registrar of Voters regarding whether non-commercial loans to candidates or committees are subject to the $250 contribution limit under AB 1400 and local ordinance, clarifying that such loans constitute contributions unless they meet specific commercial lending exceptions.
